a_issues: Content A mostly contains secondary summaries and snippets, with no result clearly providing the full text of Murmu's article. b_issues: Content B also does not directly supply the full text from the original publication, and some sources are secondary/aggregator-style (Big News Network, Devdiscourse). reasoning: B better enables an LLM to answer correctly because it contains more of the substantive facts from Murmu's article: lifelong devotion since childhood, singing bhajans, faith guiding her through personal challenges and public life, prayer at the Delhi Jagannath temple before filing nomination papers, feeling the deity's presence during oath-taking, and the barefoot walk on the Bada Danda after becoming President. These align with the anchor and reproduced text. A has only high-level summaries and less factual coverage, so it is less sufficient for answering a query seeking the article's substance or full text.
